question.text: In which year was the Securities Contracts (Regulation) Act (SCRA) amended to include derivatives as securities?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Securities Contracts (Regulation) Act (SCRA) was amended in 1999 to include derivatives within the definition of "securities." This legal amendment was essential before exchange-traded derivatives could be launched in India. **The Trap:** 1996 is when the L.C. Gupta Committee was set up. 2000 is when actual trading in index futures began. 2001 is when options and stock futures were introduced. The SCRA amendment in 1999 preceded the launch of trading. **Cross-Exam Context:** This regulatory milestone is tested in NISM Series VIII. The sequence: 1996 (committee) -> 1998 (reports) -> 1999 (SCRA amendment) -> 2000 (trading begins). **Key Takeaway:** SCRA was amended in 1999 to legally recognize derivatives as securities, paving the way for exchange-traded derivatives in India starting June 2000.
